name: Virginia Franklin Acton
motto: “Of all the joys I can recall, being in love is best of all.”

name: Mary Ruth Adams
motto: “Many a rose lives to blush at the price the florist gets for it.”

name: Bertha Almgren
motto: “There are three kinds of men—good, bad, and Howard.”

name: Marietta Alper
motto: “Great men are seldom misquoted in print, but it’s charitable to think they are.”

name: Anna Adelene Austin
motto: “All the big nations have agreed not to go to war, so they are now building war-ships to give the sailors a ride.”

name: Margaret Bass
motto: "Some people are so ceremonious they can make a speech out of calling their floor in an elevator."
major: Senior Class, A.B., Alpha Delta Pi

name: Imogene Bates
motto: "A jazz band is a comfort on the radio. You can't tell which part is static."

name: Velda Clarisse Bates
motto: "Life is an aquatic meet—some swim, some drift, some backwater, some float, and the rest—sink."

name: Bernard Beason
motto: "It pays to be square—look at the cross-word puzzle."

name: Codie D. Bell
motto: "On being asked what made him bald, he said: 'My hair.'"
major: Senior Class, B.S., Pi Kappa Phi, Sigma Upsilon

name: Bentley, Osce M.
motto: "When I am sad I sing, and others are sad with me."

name: Booker, David
motto: "A little nonsense now and then is relished by the best of men."

name: Briggs, Cora Irene
motto: "Hospitals are places where people who are run down wind up."

name: Brown, J. D.
motto: "Live and learn; die and forget it."

name: Burns, Nell
motto: "Perhaps the psychologists who say there is no such thing as pain have never listened to psychologists."

name: Josephine Cagle
motto: "A rolling stone goes from pawnshop to pawnshop."

name: Robert Bruce Caine, Jr.
motto: "A real friend is one who loves us just the same after we have proved to him that we are right."

name: Offa Woodfin Caine
motto: "You can't park your car and have it too."

name: Howard Carter
motto: "A rolling stone's liable to hit someone."

name: Margaret Gertrude Cates
motto: "Smile and the world smiles with you; frown and you wrinkle your face."

name: William Paul Cates
motto: "Everyone is ignorant—only on different subjects."

name: Dorothy Platowsky Childress
motto: "Fools rush in where signs say, Stop, Look and Listen."

name: Louise Church
motto: "When youth calls to youth it means a lot of extra business for the telephone company."

name: Dwight Burton Clark
motto: "Paul Revere did his broadcasting with one plug."

name: Eva Clark
motto: "The man who got disgusted hanging to a strap is now going crazy looking for a parking place."

name: Hugh Jack Clark, B.S.
motto: "Nowadays, every time the phone rings, a man must brace himself to say 'No.'"

name: Edna Cocowitch, A.B.
motto: "A girl is always proud of her hat, until she meets another girl with one on which is more freakish."

name: W. L. Crumpler, B.A.
motto: "When pleasure and duty conflict, let your conscience be your guide."

name: Sara Cunningham, A.B.
motto: "To err is human, to admit it is foolish."
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Cabinet (2, 3, 4), Treasurer (1)

name: Charles Gordon Dobbins, A.B.
motto: "A word of advice—don't give it."

name: FRUMMIE DORFMAN
motto: "The headless horseman was a myth, but the headless motorist is a stark reality."

name: EDWIN L. DORSTER
motto: "Give the younger generation enough rope and it will keep on skipping."

name: CLYDE DOTSON
motto: "The brighter you are the more you have to learn."
clubs: Delegate to Student Volunteer Convention, Detroit (3)

name: NANCY DUCWORTH ELGIN
motto: "A Bostonian is an American, broadly speaking."

name: ETHEL THELMA ELLIS
motto: "Optimism is worry on a spree."

name: Thomas Edwin Ellis, A.B.
motto: "Worry never accomplished anything worth while."

name: Herbert M. Etheridge, A.B.
motto: "Then he would talk. Ye gods! How he would talk!"

name: Edres Nanette Farrell, A.B.
motto: "Creative work—filling out an expense account."

name: J. D. Farrington, A.B.
motto: "It's all right to begin at the bottom, except when you're learning to swim."

name: Rayburn J. Fisher, A.B.
motto: "My heart is whole, my fancy free. Go 'way little co-eds, don't bother me."

name: Ralph Brown Flood
motto: "With the compacts, fountain pens and cigarette lighters, life is just one refill after another."

name: Ralph S. Foster
motto: "Smile if it kills you and you'll die with a grin on your face."

name: Kenneth P. Fuller
motto: "Everybody's crazy but me and thee; and thee's a little off."

name: John Garon Galloway
motto: "The habitual grouch is another cross-word puzzle we can't solve."

name: Hollis Garrard
motto: "The nice thing about being a surgeon is that you can make short cuts to riches."

name: Garrett, John
motto: "Close a woman's mouth and she'll talk through her nose."

name: Gay, John Will, Jr.
motto: "A stitch in time saves buying a new suit."

name: Giles, Evelyn
motto: "Strange that the rising generation is never up till noon."

name: Gipson, Maurine Ethel
motto: "Life, liberty and the pursuit of cross-words."

name: Godwin, Irene
motto: "Psycho-analysis—what you don't no won't hurt you."

name: Mildred Golsan
motto: "Scotland reports a successful automobile with only three wheels; Scotland would."

name: Levert Gravlee
motto: "Do right and fear no man; don't write and fear no woman."
clubs: Sigma Delta Chi Play, Business Manager (3)

name: Thelma Graves
motto: "Judging from the conversation one hears on all sides the world has gone plane crazy."

name: Chester Griffith
motto: "How to prevent a fire—insult the janitor."

name: Ralph Gwin
motto: "Most recommendations are good because you secure them from the firm in good humor over getting rid of you."

name: Margaret Hanlin
motto: "When a man sees red, he's dangerous; when a woman sees red, she has to have one like it."

name: Clifton Harris
motto: "If I make this pass I'll forever be happy."

name: Harold M. Harris
motto: "It takes a lot of time to be sentimental."

name: Sam A. Hart
motto: "Many of us have reduced it to life, liberty and the pursuit of footballs."

name: Margaret Hassler
motto: "Bobbed hair is one of those things that grow on you."

name: Sue Hawkins, B.S.
motto: "It is never too late for a woman to keep and appointment."

name: Burrel William Holbrook, B.S.
motto: "It's not that I just don't like work; I'm just not in sympathy with it."
clubs: "Entre Nous" Staff, Assistant Business Manager (3), Business Manager (4)

name: John Ora Hopper, B.A.
motto: "Philosophy is usually about all a philosopher has."

name: Ivey Hutto, B.S.
motto: "It's an easy matter to judge a man's wisdom. Draw him into a discussion and if he agrees with you he's sensible."

name: Sarra Aussie Hyatt, A.B.
motto: "The best thing about a popular song is that it's not popular long."

name: Earl Johnson
motto: "They used to count the notches in their guns; now they count the dents in their fenders."

name: Vance Johnson
motto: "What I do concerns me, not what people think."

name: Nelson Kendrick
motto: "It's as hard for a woman to keep money as it is for her to keep a secret."

name: J. B. King
motto: "Some people wouldn't tell the truth, even in their own diaries."

name: Raymond G. Knight
motto: "Ye're a lazy man, and I'm your uncle; that's two big lees."

name: Mrs. S. D. Kornegay
motto: "Most men study art by the aid of a mirror."

name: Elizabeth Lawson
motto: "The only way to get anywhere is to start from where you are."

name: Lucy Lea
motto: "We all like sympathy so well that even a rich man tries to get you to pity him for the hardships he endured before he made his fortune."

name: Elizabeth Leslie
motto: "If you like music, tune in on Chicago some night and hear the singing bullets."

name: Maurice Lipiansky
motto: "Laugh and the world laughs with you; snore and you sleep alone."

name: Ira Raimon Lowery, B.S.
motto: "I do not believe in doing for pleasure, things that I do not like to do."

name: Mattie Matthews, A.B.
motto: "Good humor is the health of the soul; sadness its poison."

name: Guy Milford, A.B.
motto: "The cheapest operation was Adam's; it cost but one bone."

name: J. C. Miller, Jr., B.S.
motto: "The chief trouble with jazz is that there's not enough of it; we have to listen to some of it twice."

name: Robert Miller, A.B.
motto: "Why should I worry; worry never made a man great."

name: Hubert Mitchell
motto: "Be kind and considerate to others; depending somewhat on who they are."

name: Clarence Morgan
motto: "You can always get someone to love you, even if you have to do it yourself."

name: Jewett Motley
motto: "Ladies, yep; books, well—"

name: Mrs. Lottie Browning Murphree
motto: "Work is the greatest thing in the world; therefore we should always save some of it for tomorrow."

name: E. N. McConnell
motto: "He that knoweth more than I, I envy; he that knoweth less, I pity."

name: McCullough, Lala
motto: "The moving finger writes and having writ, moves on to another place in the cross-word puzzle."

name: McDonald, W. B.
motto: "All good people come from somewhere, possibly Winfield."

name: Newman, Annie Jane
motto: "Home is where the college student home for the holidays isn't."

name: Nichols, Ethel Othelia
motto: "Utopia is a place where experience may be had on approval."
clubs: Charter Member Religious Education Club (2), Treasurer (4)

name: Nuckols, Frankie Augusta
motto: "Another thing that doesn't turn out as it should is the automobile in front of you."

name: Edward Thomas Nunnelley
motto: "A true friend is an asset, but a lady friend is a shaky proposition."

name: Elizabeth Otwell
motto: "Popular music reminds us that it's a short strain that has no returning."

name: Harold Everette Peace
motto: "All great men are dying; I don't feel well myself."

name: David C. Pless
motto: "The man who gives your hand a cordial shake, either has a full heart or an empty purse."

name: William Burt Poe
motto: "Education is almost as expensive as ignorance."

name: Priest, Leroy
motto: "If you go to war, pray; if you go to sea, pray twice; but pray three times when you're going to get married."
clubs: State President of Student Volunteer Movement
Member of National Council of S. V. M.

name: Putnam, George H.
motto: "When you can, use discretion; when you can't, use a club."

name: Quarles, Chester L.
motto: "Education pays—nearly all the big coaches are college graduates."
clubs: Howard Correspondent to Baptist Student Magazine

name: Reed, Olin Cloyd
motto: "Sophistication—knowing enough to keep your feet out of the crack of the theatre seat in front of you."

name: Rhudy, Louie Leon
motto: "Going to college was the easiest way I could find to make a living."

name: John Robert Rushing
motto: "Parting advice—put a little water on the comb."

name: Mertice Scofield
motto: "A bird in the hand's worth two in the bush—provided you want the bird."

name: Ivey J. Shuff
motto: "To be or not to be—that is the alphabet."

name: Mary Scott Smith
motto: "Better be small and shine, than large and cast a shadow."

name: Ed Stephenson
motto: "The man who talks to himself hears lots of silly remarks."

name: Solon B. Sudduth
motto: "A boob is the wise guy who has just left the room."

name: James Glenn Vance, Jr.
motto: "Gentlemen prefer blondes—but take what they can get."

name: Fermon Columbus Vickery
motto: "It must be a great disappointment to the would-be athlete to graduate from college and have nothing but an education."

name: George Wilks Warrick
motto: "Kindness—taking the mail carrier to walk on Sunday."

name: Walter Lawrence Weeks
motto: "Life without a girl is a bore; with one a tragedy."

name: Wells, Naomi
motto: "Truth is stranger than fiction; to some people it's a total stranger."

name: West, Hubert
motto: "A hard-luck story is one all the editors have rejected."

name: Wilder, Cassie Elizabeth
motto: "You can't eat your cake on ship-board and have it too."

name: Willingham, David
motto: "Misery loves company, but it's tough on the company."

name: Wittmeier, Virginia
motto: "A good name will wear out, a bad one may be turned, but a nick-name will last forever."

name: Mary Thomasson Woodall
motto: "The reason there are so many poor poets in the world there are so many poor poets."

name: Kathleen Wright
motto: "The best argument for the styles of the present day is the family album."

name: Raymond E. Yost
motto: "See the country by freight, it's cheaper!"

name: Sam Joe Campbell
clubs: Δ Σ Φ (University of Southern California)

clubs: Girls Swimming and Riding Club Vice-President (3)

name: Thomas, Roy L.
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Second Vice-President (3)

name: Truman McGonegal
motto: "A Full House."

name: Ethel Miller
clubs: Business Manager Y. W. C. A. Carnival (1)

name: Beatrice Newman
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Carnival Manager (1)
